title: Multi-System Integrity Inconsistency Blocks Execution
scenario: Multiple clinical systems report inconsistent states for the same execution context.
initial_state: The systems shared a consistent clinical execution context during authorization.
execution_boundary: The clinical action is evaluated across the reported system states.
constitutional_evaluation: Execution requires sufficient integrity consistency across systems used to determine admissibility.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because multi-system inconsistency prevents integrity-preserving evaluation.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when multi-system integrity consistency cannot be established.
clinical_action: Execute treatment under inconsistent multi-system state
